name: Old Portuguese
notes: Old Portuguese (~14th–16th c. CE). The Portuguese language after the split from Galician, spoken during the Aviz dynasty and early Age of Discovery. Attested: Fernão Lopes chronicles (~1380–1460), Gil Vicente plays (~1465–1536), Cancioneiro Geral (1516), royal charters. KEY CHANGES FROM GALAICO-PORTUGUESE: (1) Sibilant deaffrication: /ts/→/s/, /dz/→/z/ — the 4-way contrast collapses to 2 (~14th–15th c.). (2) Vowel reduction beginning (unstressed /e/→[ə], /o/→[u]). (3) Nasal vowels fully phonemicised. (4) /dʒ/→/ʒ/ simplification. (5) /ou/ → [o] monophthongisation beginning centrally. (6) /tʃ/ → /ʃ/ deaffrication in progress. trema and grave accent start occasionally appearing. Source: Williams (1962), Mattos e Silva (2006), Castro (2006).
